title: Fix MongoDB Pipeline to Ignore Missing Fields in Projection

statement.markdown: Given a MongoDB collection with documents containing various fields, write a function to create an aggregation pipeline that projects only the specified fields. The function should handle cases where the fields are missing from the documents.
statement.inputFormat: A MongoDB collection with documents containing various fields.
statement.outputFormat: An aggregation pipeline that projects only the specified fields.
statement.notes: The function should not raise any errors when the fields are missing from the documents.

constraints.description: Number of documents in the collection
Array of field names to project

hints.text: Use the '$project' aggregation operator to project the specified fields
Use the '$ifNull' aggregation operator to handle missing fields

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing the Aggregation Pipeline
editorial.intuition: The aggregation pipeline should project only the specified fields and handle cases where the fields are missing.
editorial.bruteForce: Use a loop to project each field individually.
editorial.betterSolution: Use the '$project' aggregation operator with the '$ifNull' operator to handle missing fields.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the '$project' aggregation operator with the '$ifNull' operator and the '$addFields' operator to handle missing fields.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The aggregation pipeline should return the documents with only the specified fields.
editorial.dryRun: Run the aggregation pipeline on a sample collection to test the solution.
